title: Macbeth
author: William Shakespeare
content: “Stars, hide your fires; let not light see my black and deep desires.”
action: Monitor the psychological point where ambition begins to override systemic stability.

title: Julius Caesar
author: William Shakespeare
content: “The fault, dear Brutus, is not in our stars, but in ourselves, that we are underlings.”
action: Reject external excuses and accept full responsibility for your current position.

title: All the King's Men
author: Robert Penn Warren
content: “Man is conceived in sin and born in corruption and he passeth from the stink of the didie to the stench of the shroud.”
action: Factor the inherent volatility of human nature into all power structure designs.

title: Things Fall Apart
author: Chinua Achebe
content: “Things fall apart; the centre cannot hold; mere anarchy is loosed upon the world.”
action: Identify the primary governing authority and maintain its structural integrity.

title: Lord of the Flies
author: William Golding
content: “The world, that understandable and lawful world, was slipping away.”
action: Prevent institutional decay to avoid a reversion to primitive, zero-sum survival protocols.
